title: Proxmox vzdump Scheduled Backup Task Failure Detection
dataSources: `index=virtualization` `sourcetype="proxmox:task"` (or Proxmox TA **Node Task** feed) with fields `task_type`, `status`, `vmid`, `upid`, `node`
app: Proxmox TA (Splunkbase app 7896), Splunk Universal Forwarder monitoring `/var/log/pve/tasks/`
spl:
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:task" task_type="vzdump" earliest=-24h
| where status!="OK" AND isnotnull(status)
| stats count as failures, values(error_message) as errors by vmid, node, upid
| sort -failures
description: Node-local vzdump jobs back up guests to file or Proxmox Backup Server targets. A failed vzdump row in the Proxmox task log means the guest was not protected on that schedule, which is often noticed only after a ransomware or corruption event.
value: Restores confidence in RPO for Proxmox guests by surfacing failed vzdump tasks immediately instead of waiting for the next DR test.
implementation: Install the Proxmox TA or forward parsed task logs from each PVE node. Normalize `task_type` to vzdump for backup jobs. Extract `status`, `vmid`, `upid`, and error text. Alert on any non-OK terminal status. Optionally join to a CMDB of guests that require daily backups.
visualization: Table (failed vzdump by VM and node), Timeline (backup task outcomes), Single value (failed jobs in last 24h).

references.title: Proxmox VE Admin Guide — Backup and Restore

knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
